x i w: noun: [x i sh] A mid-frequency radio transmission or broadcast signal.
q r i: noun: [q' r i] An active, autonomous Martas defense drone.
a r t a: noun: [a r t' a] The root execution language and underlying logic of the enclave.
j a x: verb: [k' a x] To physically strip or scavenge a hardware component.
c a c: verb: [c a c] To physically splice or bridge two live electrical wires.
f i r: verb: [f i r] To execute a continuous software process or loop.
h u w: verb: [h u sh] To place a system into a low-voltage idle or standby state.
v a w: verb: [gh a sh] To aggressively overclock a hardware component.
s i r: noun: [s i r] A physical copper wire or data bus.
t u c: noun: [t' u c] A physical, hand-held maintenance tool.
c i t: noun: [c i t'] An environmental or industrial hardware sensor.
f u x: noun: [f u x] Liquid coolant or industrial fluid used in thermal management.
q a: noun: [q' a] A physically insulated, offline sanctuary safe from network intrusion.
c i r: verb: [c i r] To fetch or read raw data from a specific memory address.
j i x: verb: [k' i x] To permanently flash or burn data onto a hardened ROM chip.
h i w: verb: [h i sh] To echo or output a raw signal through a hardware terminal.
q u t: verb: [q' u t'] To logically partition or reserve a sector of raw storage.
f a c: verb: [f a c] To securely flush a buffer or drop a software connection.
t i j: noun: [t' i k'] A logical pointer directing execution to a specific hardware address.
s u g: noun: [s u ng] A temporary data buffer or holding queue.
x a: noun: [x a] An empty, unwritten sector of memory; a null pointer.
I: modifier: [i. QUEUED] Indicates an action scheduled to happen (Future).
A: modifier: [a. ACTIVE] Indicates an action currently executing (Present).
U: modifier: [u. LOGGED] Indicates an action that has concluded and written to memory (Past).
C: modifier: [c. IF GATE] Evaluates the following phrase as a condition.
X: modifier: [x. NOT GATE] Inverts or nullifies the following opcode or register.
S: modifier: [s. VECTOR ABOVE] Indicates location above, on top of, or at a higher physical elevation.
M: prefix: [m. HOST] Binds an opcode to the speaker (I/Me), or assigns local possession (My).
N: prefix: [n. CLUSTER] Binds an opcode to the speaker's group (We), or assigns group possession (Our).
K: prefix: [k. CLIENT] Binds an opcode to the target (You), or assigns target possession (Your).
W: prefix: [sh. EXTERNAL] Binds an opcode to a third-party, or assigns external possession.
F: modifier: [f. VECTOR IN / THIS] Indicates movement inside, or acts as a local memory pointer.
H: modifier: [h. VECTOR OUT / THAT] Indicates movement outside, or acts as an external memory pointer.
G: modifier: [ng. VECTOR BELOW] Indicates location below, underneath, or grounded.
T: modifier: [t'. VECTOR AT] Indicates an exact, physical connection or adjacent location.
Q: modifier: [q'. QUERY] Casts the following phrase as a polling request for data (Question).
R: modifier: [r. OR GATE] Offers a diverging branch between two choices.
V: modifier: [gh. AND GATE] Binds two conditions or opcodes together on a single logical track.
modifier: [EXECUTION TRIGGER] The Hardware Bus. Acts as a 'THEN' gate.
modifier: [SYSTEM HALT / EOF] End of File. Clears all active thread contexts.
